headline: OpenAI closes reasoning gap in voice agents
subtitle: GPT-Realtime models boost voice AI reasoning · Google integrates Fitbit into health platform · Test multiple AI models side-by-side
intro: OpenAI's new voice models bring significant reasoning upgrades to AI agents, enabling them to think while speaking and handle multiple tasks simultaneously. Meanwhile, Google is consolidating health data into a unified platform, and practical tools emerge for comparing AI model performance.

bullets.text: OpenAI launches GPT-Realtime-2 with advanced reasoning for voice agents
bullets.summary: OpenAI released three voice models that bring GPT-5-level reasoning to live speech, enabling simultaneous tool use and thinking while conversing. Performance improved 15 points on Big Bench Audio benchmarks, and early adopters like Zillow and Priceline are already building applications with the technology.

bullets.text: Google consolidates Fitbit into unified health platform with AI coach
bullets.summary: Google integrated Fitbit into a new Health platform powered by Gemini, offering AI-driven workout routines and medical record interpretation. The company introduced a $99 screenless Fitbit tracker and plans to expand AI coach access to third-party wearables like Apple Watch and Garmin.

bullets.text: OpenRouter Fusion enables side-by-side testing of multiple AI models
bullets.summary: OpenRouter Fusion lets users compare outputs from different AI models using identical prompts simultaneously. The tool helps identify which model performs best for specific tasks while tracking costs and model performance metrics.

bullets.text: Anthropic Institute publishes research agenda on self-improving AI systems
bullets.summary: Anthropic's research arm released a formal agenda addressing security, governance, and economic implications of self-improving AI. The institute proposes Cold War-style protocols between labs and governments plus fire drills for handling capability surges.

bullets.text: Spotify launches Personal Podcasts feature powered by AI agents
bullets.summary: Spotify introduced a tool that transforms briefings and class notes into personalized podcasts within users' libraries. The feature leverages AI agents to convert written content into audio format automatically.

bullets.text: OpenAI introduces Trusted Contact feature for ChatGPT safety alerts
bullets.summary: ChatGPT now includes an optional Trusted Contact feature that notifies designated contacts if the system detects potential self-harm risks. This opt-in safety measure provides an additional layer of support for vulnerable users.

bullets.text: OpenAI's Codex integrates directly into Chrome on macOS and Windows
bullets.summary: Codex functionality is now available as a native Chrome extension, allowing developers to write and test code directly within the browser without leaving their editing environment.

bullets.text: OpenAI releases new realtime audio models for API users
bullets.summary: The new audio models enable low-latency voice interactions, expanding OpenAI's capabilities beyond text-based applications into conversational AI scenarios.

bullets.text: GitHub improves token efficiency in agentic workflow systems
bullets.summary: GitHub shares optimization techniques that reduce token consumption in AI agent workflows, lowering costs and improving response times for developers using Copilot.

bullets.text: Researchers document observations from inside China's top AI labs
bullets.summary: A detailed analysis provides rare insights into the research directions, infrastructure approaches, and organizational structures of leading Chinese AI laboratories.

bullets.text: Anthropic publishes research on natural language autoencoders
bullets.summary: The research explores how autoencoders can be applied to language models, potentially improving model interpretability and performance through better representation learning.

bullets.text: Google DeepMind partners with EVE Online for AI testing
bullets.summary: The partnership uses EVE Online's complex multiplayer environment as a testbed for developing and evaluating advanced AI agents in realistic decision-making scenarios.

bullets.text: ChatGPT free tier upgraded to GPT-5.5 Instant model
bullets.summary: Free ChatGPT users now access GPT-5.5 Instant, featuring improved vision capabilities, better PDF understanding, and smarter web search. The model produces shorter responses with fewer emojis and hallucinates 52.5% less on high-stakes prompts compared to its predecessor.

bullets.text: Claude usage limits doubled via SpaceX Colossus partnership
bullets.summary: Anthropic doubled token limits across all Claude paid plans by signing a deal to use SpaceX's Colossus 1 data center. This expansion enables more substantial agentic workloads and higher-throughput applications.

bullets.text: ChatGPT now integrates with Excel and Google Sheets
bullets.summary: ChatGPT can now build sheets, analyze tabs, write formulas and make approved edits directly within Excel and Google Sheets. This integration streamlines data work without leaving the spreadsheet environment.

bullets.text: Claude Managed Agents adds dreaming and multi-agent orchestration
bullets.summary: New Claude Managed Agents features include Dreaming to review and save memories from past chats, Outcomes for defining and grading success criteria, and multi-agent orchestration allowing lead agents to delegate to specialists.

bullets.text: PostHog building Codex-like code editor with product data signals
bullets.summary: PostHog is developing a code editor using product usage patterns, bugs and errors as primary signals for building and coding. This represents a shift toward self-driving product loops that leverage telemetry data.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases 10 finance agent templates for enterprises
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched ten finance agent templates covering pitchbooks, KYC screening, valuation reviews and month-end close processes. These templates run as Claude plugins or Managed Agents cookbooks for financial workflows.

bullets.text: Apple's camera AirPods reach late-stage development testing
bullets.summary: Apple's unreleased AirPods with cameras (codename 'Glow') have entered late-stage testing. The cameras are designed to feed Apple Intelligence visual context from the wearer's environment, enabling richer contextual AI assistance.

bullets.text: Cloudflare cuts 1,100 jobs in AI-first restructuring push
bullets.summary: CEO Matthew Prince announced the restructuring as AI-driven. Revenue per employee has climbed roughly 600% over three years on productivity gains from AI adoption, showing the efficiency gains motivating the job cuts.

bullets.text: DeepMind's AlphaEvolve reports 23 verified scientific discoveries
bullets.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve scaled to real-world impact with 23 verified discoveries across chemistry, materials, and applied mathematics in the past quarter, demonstrating concrete scientific contributions from AI research systems.

bullets.text: ElevenLabs slashes voice synthesis pricing by 40% across tiers
bullets.summary: The text notes the pricing cut includes multilingual Turbo model options, with the newsletter questioning the timing motivation behind the aggressive price reduction.

main_story.title: OpenAI's voice models bring advanced reasoning to live speech
main_story.body: OpenAI introduced GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper, three API models designed to dramatically improve how AI agents handle voice interactions. The new Realtime-2 model brings GPT-5-level reasoning capabilities directly to live speech, enabling systems to think while speaking, use multiple tools simultaneously, and maintain natural conversational flow. Performance metrics show a 15-point improvement on Big Bench Audio benchmarks, jumping from 81.4% to 96.6%. The suite includes a live translator supporting over 70 languages and a streaming transcription model, creating a comprehensive toolkit for voice-based AI applications. Major companies including Zillow, Priceline, and Deutsche Telekom are already building production applications using these models for real estate agents, travel management, and customer support.
main_story.why: Voice represents the next frontier for AI agent deployment, requiring fundamentally different capabilities than text-based systems. These reasoning improvements move voice AI from simple turn-based interactions to sophisticated agents that can handle complex workflows at the speed of natural conversation.
main_story.take: The AI industry's focus on text agents may have been a limiting constraint. Voice is where agents must prove they can operate in the messier, more fluid interface of real human communication, and OpenAI's advances suggest the industry is finally closing that gap.

around_the_horn.text: Scale AI secured $500M Pentagon contract for military data analysis
Perplexity launched Personal Computer feature for Mac users
Mozilla reported Claude Mythos Preview patched more bugs than previous 15 months

previous.headline: Anthropic, SpaceX(AI) become unlikely compute partners
previous.subtitle: Anthropic leases SpaceX's Colossus 1 cluster · Mira Murati testifies against Altman · DeepMind invests in EVE Online
previous.intro: In a stunning reversal months after calling Anthropic "Misanthropic," Elon Musk is renting them his entire Colossus 1 supercomputer cluster. The deal simultaneously solves Claude's compute constraints, strengthens Musk's rival against OpenAI, and signals a new business opportunity for SpaceX's AI division.

bullets.text: Anthropic leases SpaceX's 220K-GPU Colossus 1 cluster
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed a deal to lease SpaceX's Colossus 1 supercomputer in Memphis, which features over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs coming online within weeks. The partnership enables Anthropic to double Claude Code's usage caps and remove peak-hour restrictions across paid tiers. Musk has signaled SpaceX will rent compute to AI companies taking responsible steps toward safe AI development.

bullets.text: Mira Murati accuses Sam Altman of lying in trial
bullets.summary: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati testified in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming CEO Sam Altman lied about a model's safety review and created chaos by giving conflicting directions to different executives. She also described how Altman's actions undermined her authority and put the company at risk of collapse during the 2023 board crisis.

bullets.text: DeepMind invests in Fenris, uses EVE Online for AI research
bullets.summary: Google DeepMind acquired a minority stake in Fenris Creations, the studio behind EVE Online, to use the 23-year-old space MMO as a sandbox for AI research. The game's complex player-driven economy and long-timeline decision-making provide ideal conditions for testing how AI agents learn, retain memory, and strategize in unpredictable environments.

bullets.text: Anthropic adds dreaming and orchestration to Managed Agents
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched new features for Claude Managed Agents, including a dreaming capability that lets agents review past sessions, outcomes-based grading for work quality, and multi-agent orchestration for splitting complex tasks. These additions help agents learn from history and coordinate across multiple specialized systems.

bullets.text: Subquadratic launches SubQ with 12M token context window
bullets.summary: Subquadratic debuted SubQ, a model featuring a 12-million-token context window and claiming a 52x speed boost on long-context tasks compared to competitors at a fraction of the cost. The breakthrough addresses practical challenges in handling extremely long documents and sequences efficiently.

bullets.text: Claude adds self-improving agent capabilities
bullets.summary: Claude now supports self-improving agents that can autonomously refine their behavior and capabilities. This advancement enables more sophisticated autonomous workflows and represents a significant step toward more independent AI systems.

bullets.text: Anthropic announces SpaceX compute partnership
bullets.summary: Anthropic has established a compute deal with SpaceX alongside raising usage limits for Claude. The partnership provides significant computational infrastructure to support scaling of Anthropic's AI models and services.

bullets.text: ProgramBench launches AI programming benchmark
bullets.summary: A new benchmark for evaluating AI performance on programming tasks has been released. ProgramBench provides standardized metrics to assess how well language models handle real-world coding challenges.

bullets.text: DeepSeek attracts $50 billion investment from China
bullets.summary: China is investing significantly in DeepSeek, valuing the AI company at $50 billion. This move signals strong government backing for domestic AI development and competitive positioning in the global AI race.

bullets.text: TokenSpeed accelerates AI inference for agents
bullets.summary: A new inference engine called TokenSpeed optimizes AI model execution for agentic workloads. The technology aims to deliver high-speed token generation critical for responsive autonomous agent systems.

bullets.text: Harvey launches legal AI agent benchmark
bullets.summary: Harvey has introduced a specialized benchmark for evaluating legal AI agents. The metric helps assess how well AI systems perform on domain-specific legal tasks and reasoning.

bullets.text: Free ChatGPT users upgrade to GPT-5.5 Instant model
bullets.summary: OpenAI replaced GPT-5.3 Instant with GPT-5.5 Instant for free users, featuring significantly better vision capabilities, PDF understanding, web search integration, and memory handling. The new model reduces hallucinations by 52.5% on high-stakes prompts and generates shorter, emoji-light responses.

bullets.text: Anthropic doubles Claude token limits with SpaceX deal
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed an agreement to use all of SpaceX's Colossus 1 data center, enabling twice as much Claude usage across all paid plans. This infrastructure move significantly expands capacity for users running complex agentic workflows.

bullets.text: ChatGPT now integrates directly into Excel and Sheets
bullets.summary: ChatGPT extended its functionality to spreadsheet applications, allowing users to build sheets, analyze tabs, write formulas, and make approved edits directly within Excel and Google Sheets without leaving the interface.

bullets.text: PostHog builds Codex-like editor for self-driving product loop
bullets.summary: PostHog is creating a Codex-like application that uses product data as primary signals—usage patterns, observed bugs, and log errors—to automatically generate and improve code. The approach treats product understanding as the foundation for agentic development.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases 10 finance agent templates for enterprises
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched pre-built finance agent templates covering pitchbooks, KYC screening, valuation reviews, and month-end close processes. These run as Claude Cowork plugins or Managed Agent cookbooks, enabling rapid deployment for financial teams.

bullets.text: Anthropic partners with SpaceX for 220K+ GPUs and 300MW Memphis data center
bullets.summary: Anthropic signed a partnership with SpaceX to gain full access to Colossus 1, a 300+ MW data center in Memphis (formerly xAI's campus). The deal brings 220,000+ NVIDIA GPUs online within the month, dramatically expanding Anthropic's compute capacity.

bullets.text: Claude Code rate limits doubled; peak-hour restrictions lifted for Pro/Max
bullets.summary: Five-hour rate limits for Claude Code doubled across Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans. Peak-hours limit reductions were eliminated for Pro and Max users, addressing the performance bottlenecks that plagued the platform over the past six weeks.

bullets.text: Claude briefly went down during keynote despite announcing compute fixes
bullets.summary: During Code with Claude SF, Claude experienced downtime for thousands of users around 11:16 AM ET, ironically coinciding with keynote announcements about massive capacity additions. The service restored within the hour.

main_story.title: Anthropic and SpaceX forge unexpected compute partnership
main_story.body: Anthropic has secured a landmark deal to lease SpaceX's Colossus 1 supercomputer cluster, a 300-plus megawatt facility in Memphis housing over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs. The partnership enables Anthropic to immediately double Claude Code's usage limits across paid tiers and eliminates peak-hour restrictions that previously constrained users. Within the same week, The Information also reported that Anthropic committed to a $200 billion, 5-gigawatt compute deal with Google Cloud over five years, indicating aggressive expansion plans. Musk signaled on X that SpaceX plans to rent compute capacity to other AI companies pursuing responsible development, positioning SpaceX as an emerging compute landlord even as its Grok model continues chasing frontier performance.
main_story.why: This partnership is remarkable for its strategic layering: Musk addresses Anthropic's severe compute bottleneck, simultaneously strengthens Claude against OpenAI (his stated competitor), and opens a new revenue stream for SpaceX. The shift reveals how compute scarcity has become the bottleneck determining AI leadership.
main_story.take: The deal underscores that even bitter rivals in AI will collaborate when compute access is the limiting factor. Musk's willingness to empower Claude despite public criticism of Anthropic demonstrates that compute landlordship may transcend ideological divisions in the race to build powerful AI systems.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI partners with AMD, Intel, NVIDIA on MRC to prevent hardware failures during training
Chinese startup DeepSeek nearing $45B valuation in new funding round
Google partners with Believe to integrate Flow Music and Lyria 3 Pro for artists
